prs.title: fix: bump langchain deps to v1
prs.body:
Fixes #860

## Summary

Bumps `@langchain/core` and `@langchain/openai` from `^0.3.0` to `^1.0.0` in `examples/typescript/langchain` to fix `ERR_PACKAGE_PATH_NOT_EXPORTED`.

## Type of Change

- [x] fix - A bug fix

## Details

`@langchain/langgraph@1.x` imports subpaths only present in `@langchain/core@1.x`. The old `^0.3.0` pins resolved to `0.3.80`, causing the crash.

## Screenshots / Recordings (if applicable)

N/A

## Checklist

- [x] I have read the [CONTRIBUTING.md](../CONTRIBUTING.md)
- [ ] I have added/updated tests where applicable
- [ ] I have added screenshots or recordings for UI changes (if applicable)
- [ ] I have updated documentation where necessary

<!-- This is an auto-generated description by cubic. -->
---
## Summary by cubic
Upgrade `@langchain/core` and `@langchain/openai` to v1 in the TypeScript LangChain example to resolve ERR_PACKAGE_PATH_NOT_EXPORTED. Also add OpenRouter tool-agent examples for TypeScript and Python with TraceRoot instrumentation.

- New Features
  - Add ReAct-style OpenRouter agent examples with tool calling and streaming in `examples/typescript/openrouter` and `examples/python/openrouter-tool-agent` (uses OpenAI SDK pointed at OpenRouter; TraceRoot captures calls automatically).
  - Include `.env.example`, quick-start scripts (`pnpm demo`, `uv run`), and model selection notes.

- Dependencies
  - In `examples/typescript/langchain`, bump `@langchain/core` and `@langchain/openai` to `^1.0.0` to match `@langchain/langgraph@^1.0.0` subpath imports and fix `ERR_PACKAGE_PATH_NOT_EXPORTED` (fixes #860).

<sup>Written for commit 766fa6cb3611c76f1c07a91b53b0471494a775b0. Summary will update on new commits.</sup>

<!-- End of auto-generated description by cubic. -->

prs.title: feat(examples): add OpenRouter examples (Python + TypeScript)
prs.body:
Fixes #742

## Summary

Adds OpenRouter examples for both Python and TypeScript. OpenRouter is OpenAI-compatible, so TraceRoot's existing OpenAI integration captures all calls automatically — no SDK changes needed.

## Type of Change

- [x] feat - A new feature

## Details

**Python** (`examples/python/openrouter-tool-agent/`):
- ReAct agent using `openai` SDK with `base_url="https://openrouter.ai/api/v1"`
- Defaults to `anthropic/claude-3-5-sonnet`, easily configurable
- Includes `.env.example`, `main.py`, `requirements.txt`, `README.md`

**TypeScript** (`examples/typescript/openrouter/`):
- Same pattern using `openai` npm SDK with `baseURL`
- Includes `.env.example`, `agent.ts`, `package.json`, `tsconfig.json`, `README.md`

Both examples verified:
- Python imports and bootstraps cleanly
- TypeScript passes `tsc --noEmit` with no errors
- Pre-commit hooks pass

## Screenshots / Recordings (if applicable)

N/A — example code only.

## Checklist

- [x] I have read the [CONTRIBUTING.md](../CONTRIBUTING.md)
- [ ] I have added/updated tests where applicable
- [x] I have added screenshots or recordings for UI changes (if applicable)
- [x] I have updated documentation where necessary

<!-- This is an auto-generated description by cubic. -->
---
## Summary by cubic
Adds Python and TypeScript examples for using OpenRouter with the OpenAI SDK and TraceRoot; calls are captured automatically via TraceRoot’s OpenAI integration. Also fixes timezone handling and adds validation for tool arguments in the demos.

- **New Features**
  - Python example (`examples/python/openrouter-tool-agent/`): ReAct tool agent using `openai` with `base_url=https://openrouter.ai/api/v1`, defaults to `anthropic/claude-3-5-sonnet`, includes `.env.example`, `main.py`, `requirements.txt`, `README.md`.
  - TypeScript example (`examples/typescript/openrouter/`): Same pattern using `openai` and `@traceroot-ai/traceroot` with `baseURL`, includes `.env.example`, `agent.ts`, `package.json`, `tsconfig.json`, `README.md` (run with `pnpm demo`).

- **Bug Fixes**
  - Correct timezone handling in the time tool.
  - Guard against malformed tool arguments to prevent crashes.

<sup>Written for commit b31d8b138b3b1388750e31e5e8aed5e7c90ea191. Summary will update on new commits.</sup>

<!-- End of auto-generated description by cubic. -->

prs.title: fix(docker): add DATABASE_URL to worker service in prod compose
prs.body:
Fixes #835

## Summary

Adds missing `DATABASE_URL` environment variable to the `worker` service in `docker-compose.prod.yml`. Without it, the worker cannot connect to PostgreSQL for model pricing lookups, causing all trace costs to display as "-".

## Type of Change

- [x] fix - A bug fix

## Details

The worker service uses `DATABASE_URL` to query the `standard_models` and `standard_model_prices` tables in PostgreSQL for LLM cost calculation. This variable was already present in `rest`, `web`, `billing`, and `agent` services but missing from `worker`.

## Screenshots / Recordings (if applicable)

N/A — config-only change.

## Checklist

- [x] I have read the [CONTRIBUTING.md](../CONTRIBUTING.md)
- [ ] I have added/updated tests where applicable
- [x] I have added screenshots or recordings for UI changes (if applicable)
- [x] I have updated documentation where necessary

<!-- This is an auto-generated description by cubic. -->
---
## Summary by cubic
Added `DATABASE_URL` to the `worker` service in `docker-compose.prod.yml` so the worker can connect to PostgreSQL and calculate model pricing. This fixes trace costs showing "-" when the worker couldn’t fetch prices.

<sup>Written for commit ecdacad85c9aa5278a8fb2e222a1d0b3ebb9ece0. Summary will update on new commits.</sup>

<!-- End of auto-generated description by cubic. -->

prs.title: Fix: cast id parameter based on schema _id type in DocumentArray.id()
Fix persistence error calculation to handle dropped values in site data
prs.body:
# Pull Request

## Description

Fixes a bug in persistence error calculation when dropout is applied to historical PV site data. Previously, the persistence baseline would use dropped values (-1), causing huge errors in wandb metrics. The fix finds the last valid (non-dropped) value before the forecast period instead of naively using the last index position.

Fixes #485

## How Has This Been Tested?

Created a test script that verifies the persistence calculation logic works correctly:
1. Normal case: Last value before forecast is valid - ✓ PASS
2. Dropout case: Last value is dropped (-1) - ✓ PASS - correctly finds previous valid value  
3. Multiple drops: Multiple dropped values in history - ✓ PASS - correctly finds last valid value

All tests passed. Existing end-to-end tests continue to pass, confirming no regression.

- [x] Yes

## Checklist:

- [x] My code follows [OCF's coding style guidelines](https://github.com/openclimatefix/.github/blob/main/coding_style.md)

- [x] I have performed a self-review of my own code

- [ ] I have made corresponding changes to the documentation

- [ ] I have added tests that prove my fix is effective or that my feature works

- [x] I have checked my code and corrected any misspellings

prs.title: Added descriptive error messages to assert statements in LateFusionModel.__init__
prs.body:
Makes debugging config issues easier by showing exactly what's missing

# Pull Request

## Description

Added descriptive error messages to assert statements in LateFusionModel.__init__ method. Previously, when configuration parameters were missing, users would only see generic `AssertionError()` messages which made debugging very difficult. Now users get clear, specific error messages that tell them exactly what configuration parameter is missing or misconfigured.


Fixes #469

## How Has This Been Tested?

- Verified that all assert statements now have descriptive error messages
- Confirmed code follows OCF coding style guidelines (100 char line limit)
- Ran ruff linting to ensure no style violations
- Verified Python syntax compilation passes

- [x] Yes

_If your changes affect data processing, have you plotted any changes? i.e. have you done a quick sanity check?_

- [x] Yes (no data processing changes, only error message improvements)

## Checklist:

prs.title: Remove phone number from public user schedule list api
prs.body:
# Refactor UserBaseSpec and add PublicUserReadSpec

- Removed the phone_number field from UserBaseSpec.
- Added PublicUserReadSpec.

Closes #2904

## Proposed Changes

- Removed the `phone_number` field from `UserBaseSpec`.
- Added `PublicUserReadSpec` to handle publicly accessible user APIs.

### Associated Issue

- [#2904](https://github.com/Lex-Ashu/care/issues/2904): This issue required removing the `phone_number` field from `UserBaseSpec` and adding a new `PublicUserReadSpec` class to handle public user API specifications. The proposed changes solve this issue by refactoring the `UserBaseSpec` and introducing the new `PublicUserReadSpec`.

## Merge Checklist

- [] Tests added/fixed
- [] Update docs in `/docs`
- [x] Linting Complete
- [] Any other necessary step

@ohcnetwork/care-backend-maintainers @ohcnetwork/care-backend-admins

<!-- This is an auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->
## Summary by CodeRabbit

- **New Features**
  - Reintroduced the `phone_number` field for user specifications with a maximum length constraint.
  - Added a new `PublicUserReadSpec` class that includes essential user details such as last login, profile picture URL, user type, gender, and username.

- **Refactor**
  - Updated the data model for reading user information in the facility scheduling context to utilize the new `PublicUserReadSpec`.
<!-- end of auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->

prs.title: Main
prs.body:
- Replaced render_markdown_path with markdown.Markdown for simpler and more efficient rendering of markdown content. This prevents the double translation of Jinja2 code within markdown links.

- Added --admins-only Option:
  - Enhanced the management command to include a --admins-only argument, allowing emails to be sent exclusively to administrators.

- Refactored send_custom_email Function:
  - Moved the send_custom_email function to zerver/lib/send_email.py for better maintainability.
  - Added unit tests for the send_custom_email function in zerver/tests/test_email_notifications.py.

- Support for Email Headers in Markdown File:
  - Added support for including From: address and Subject: headers directly in the markdown file, ensuring a more flexible email template management.

These refinements improve the maintainability, flexibility, and efficiency of the email handling processes."

prs.title: Update main.py
prs.body:
### Title
Enhance Error Handling in Reporting Service

### Purpose
This PR improves error handling in the reporting service by implementing more granular logging and handling specific exceptions.

### Changes Made
- Added error logging for database connection failures.
- Implemented specific exception handling for `SQLAlchemyError`.
- Included warnings for cases with no data returned from queries.
- Ensured all exceptions are logged with stack traces for easier debugging.

### Testing
Tested with multiple report configurations to ensure that errors are logged correctly and reports are generated as expected.

### Impact
No changes to existing report generation logic; only improvements in error visibility and handling.

### Related Issues
Related to issue #123: Improve logging for reporting service errors.

issues.title: DocumentArray.id() doesn't properly cast id parameter for custom _id types (String, Number, etc.)
issues.body:
### Prerequisites

- [x] I have written a descriptive issue title
- [x] I have searched existing issues to ensure the bug has not already been reported


### Mongoose version

8.19.3

### Node.js version

v24.2.0

### MongoDB server version

6.0

### Typescript version (if applicable)

_No response_

### Description

The `DocumentArray.id()` method always attempts to cast the `id` parameter to ObjectId, but it should cast based on the schema's `_id` type. This causes issues when using custom `_id` types like String, Number, or Object.

**Location:** `lib/types/documentArray/methods/index.js:124` - There's a TODO comment indicating this needs to be fixed: `@TODO cast to the _id based on schema for proper comparison`

**Current Behavior:** When calling `array.id(someId)` on a DocumentArray where documents have String or Number `_id` types, the method may not find matching documents correctly because it tries to cast everything to ObjectId first.

**Expected Behavior:** The `id()` method should:
1. Get the schema's `_id` type from the document array schema
2. Cast the input `id` parameter to match that type
3. Then perform the comparison

This is a bug because the method doesn't work correctly with non-ObjectId `_id` types, even though Mongoose supports them.

### Steps to Reproduce

1. Create a schema with custom String `_id`:pt
const CustomSchema = new Schema({
  _id: { type: String, required: true },
  name: String
});
const ParentSchema = new Schema({ children: [CustomSchema] });
const Parent = mongoose.model('Parent', ParentSchema);2. Create document and try to find child by id:
const parent = new Parent({ children: [{ _id: 'abc123', name: 'test' }] });
await parent.save();

const found = parent.children.id('abc123');
// May return null because method tries to cast to ObjectId instead of String


## Expected Behavior
The `id()` method should:
1. Detect the schema's `_id` type (String, Number, Object, ObjectId, etc.)
2. Cast the input `id` parameter to match that type using the schema's cast method
3. Perform the comparison using the properly casted value

This would make the method work correctly with all `_id` types that Mongoose supports, not just ObjectId.

**Note:** There's already a TODO comment in the code at line 124 of `lib/types/documentArray/methods/index.js` that acknowledges this needs to be fixed.
